keyEvidence.finding: PIT gate exists (date: { lte: asOfDb })
sync stores ISO format (parseTwseDateToIso in getDailyStocks)
gate uses YYYYMMDD (asOf.replace(/-/g, '')) — mismatch with ISO storage
same-year future ISO dates pass YYYYMMDD filter (latent risk)
no future-labeled fields in data schema
no confirmed future-data contamination in current DB

Quote.reason: PIT gate exists but may be ineffective due to date format mismatch between storage (ISO) and gate comparison (YYYYMMDD). Same-year future records would pass the gate if present. No confirmed contamination but latent risk.
Quote.riskSummary: Gate is structurally present but potentially non-functional for same-year future data filtering. Repair required before any simulation use.
Quote.recommendedAction: Verify actual StockQuote.date format in DB. Fix gate to use consistent format. Add integration test for same-year future record exclusion.

keyEvidence.finding: detectRegime(asOf) PIT gate: date: { lte: asOf } (ISO direct)
MarketIndex.date stored as ISO (from parseTwseDateToIso via openapiDate)
ISO ≤ ISO comparison is correct — no format mismatch
all calculations are backward-looking (MA50, MA200, momentum, volatility)
detectRegimeForPeriod is backtest-only, not in scoring path
asOf correctly propagated through SignalFusionEngine
no future-labeled fields in MarketIndex schema

Regime.reason: detectRegime(asOf) uses ISO asOf directly with ISO stored MarketIndex.date. Format is consistent — correct lexicographic comparison. All regime calculations are backward-looking from PIT-gated data. detectRegimeForPeriod is backtest-only.
Regime.riskSummary: No PIT issues found in regime calculation path. Gate is correctly implemented and functional.
Regime.recommendedAction: Document ISO format assumption for MarketIndex.date. Add advisory note about detectRegimeForPeriod being backtest-only in P29A registry.

keyEvidence.finding: PIT gate exists (date: { lte: asOfDb })
sync CONFIRMED to store ISO format (date: isoDate)
schema comment 'YYYYMMDD' is WRONG — actual storage is ISO
gate uses YYYYMMDD (asOf.replace(/-/g, '')) — same mismatch as Quote
calculateChipStrength uses backward-looking aggregates only (totalBuy, foreignBuy, trustBuy)
publication lag: chip for T published at ~6pm T (end-of-day, industry standard)
no confirmed future-data contamination in current DB

Chip.reason: Same date format mismatch as Quote — sync stores ISO but gate uses YYYYMMDD. Schema comment is incorrect. Same-year future chip records would pass gate. Publication lag is acceptable for end-of-day scoring but should be documented.
Chip.riskSummary: Gate is structurally present but may be non-functional for same-year future chip data filtering. Schema documentation is wrong. Repair required.
Chip.recommendedAction: 1. Fix schema comment from YYYYMMDD to ISO. 2. Fix gate to use ISO asOf directly (same fix as Quote). 3. Document publication lag assumption. 4. Add integration test.
